{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''[Dakerno]'' witnessed the ritualistic tribal customs of two warring factions—the Farangs and the Waroots. Impressed by the natives' centuries-old tactics, Dakerno created the holo game.|Gundark's Gear Datalog|<ref name="Gundark">''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]''</ref>}}
The '''Waroot''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ab'Bshingh]]. They [[Ab'Bshingh civil war|warred]] with another indigenous species, the [[Farang]], for [[Time/Legends|centuries]], developing unique tactics and customs. Those tactics were studied by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] strategist [[Crat Dakerno]], who used them as the basis for the [[holo boardgame]] [[B'shingh]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Waroot were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to [[Ab'Bshingh]],<ref name="SWAJ" /> a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Sujimis sector/Legends|Sujimis sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Atlas">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref>

==History==
[[File:Bshingh-RidersOfTheMaelstrom.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A game of B'shingh featuring Waroot and Farangs.]]
The Waroot were at [[Ab'Bshingh civil war|war]] for [[Time/Legends|centuries]] with a second species native to the planet, the [[Farang]]s. Those two species developed unique ritualistic tribal customs and tactics over the many [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of conflict. Eventually, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] dispatched forces to quell the violence on the planet.<ref name="SWAJ" />

Among the [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] that accompanied the [[Troop/Legends|troops]] was military strategist [[Crat Dakerno]]. Dakerno was impressed by the natives' tactics and studied their customs. The strategist's analysis led him to create [[B'shingh]], a [[holo boardgame]] that became popular among certain intellectual and military elite. The game involved moving Waroot and Farangs around the board in an attempt to capture the opponent's base. However, proficiency in the game required familiarity with the ancient customs of the Waroot and their opponents, so the game never attained wide popularity.<ref name="SWAJ" /> A B'shingh hologame was manufactured by [[Dakerno Inc.|Dakerno Incorporated]] and retailed for 500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="Gundark" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|I told Charlene to write a story involving this scene so I could feature the color artwork in the Journal. She went to work and submitted "A Certain Point of View," in the plot of which she managed to highlight several elements of the painting.|Peter M. Schweighofer|<ref name="Introduction">''[[Tales from the Empire]]'', "Introduction: A Galaxy Filled with Stories"</ref>}}
The Waroot were created by [[Charlene Newcomb]] for her short story [[A Certain Point of View (short story)|''A Certain Point of View'']], published in [[West End Games]]' [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 8|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 8]] in [[1995]]. Newcomb wrote the story following a challenge from editor [[Peter M. Schweighofer]] to construct a story around the cover image of the [[1989]] adventure [[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']].<ref name="Introduction" /> The cover, illustrated by [[Daniel Horne]], featured a holographic board game, which Newcomb incorporated in the form of the Waroot and Farangs. While several figures can be discerned in the cover image, there has been no determination as to which is a Farang and which is a Waroot.

Part of the information from ''A Certain Point of View'' was later republished in the sourcebook ''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]'' in [[1997]], while the story itself was republished in ''[[Tales from the Empire]]'' in [[1997]] and on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] in [[2006]]. The Waroot also received an entry in [[2009]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
